# **\--Documentation Notation--**

* See **Races** for species considered at near or higher than Human intelligence.  
* See **Beasts** for mildly intelligent species or highly dangerous beasts.  
* See **Fauna** for low-intelligence species.  
* This list is not complete; rather, it includes species that impact the economy/story to a greater extent.  
* Adventurers hunting Fauna/Beats butchers the corpses prior to hauling in order to reduce cost.   
* See Economy Calculator for Prices.

# **\--Distance Cost Tier--**

* # **Tier I (Cheap Goods)**

  * Freshly killed animals \+ elemental interface actions for prolonged  storage

* # **Tier II (Common Goods)**

  * Processed meat \+ elemental interface actions for prolonged storage  
  * Feathers, Fur of large animals, other animal parts.

* # **Tier III (Expensive Goods)**

  * Jerky \+ elemental interface actions for storage  
  * Feathers, Fur of smaller animals, specially of higher quality  
  * Live beasts  
  * Rare animal parts.

* # **Tier IV (Nobility Goods)**

  * High-quality hides/furs, especially from rare game.

# **\--Aggressive--**

# **Hash {X}**

* **Website Description**  
  * ?  
* **Isekai History**  
  * Bigger & more veracious version of {Fauna}, {Beasts}, {Races} that lived in the Hifuna for long enough.  
  * Usually with fallen hair, bigger size, some body parts bigger/smaller than others.  
* **Scientific History**  
  * Radiation Mutation  
* **Habitat**  
  * Hifuna, while some make their way down to lower terrains and cause havoc

* ## **Economic Value**

  * ### **Extermination**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Usually, single pieces of it (often the head) are torn off and brought back to prove the hunt was completed, because the meat and other body parts are usually too irradiated to be of any use.  
    * **Cost**  
      * See Embassy Quests for more details.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * ![][image1]  
* **Tastes Like**  
  * See the originating species.   
  * Irradiated foods repeatedly smell rotten, metallic, bloody, burnt, grassy, and generally off.   
  * The taste is described as sulfur, singed hair, burnt feathers, burnt oil, and rancid fat.   
  * Meats can turn green, brown, red, or yellow.   
  * Some give off a yellow saliva-like excretion  
  * [https://www.centerforfoodsafety.org/files/food\_irradiation\_gross\_failure.pdf](https://www.centerforfoodsafety.org/files/food_irradiation_gross_failure.pdf)  
* **Alimentation**  
  * See the originating species. Often, other Hifuna species accumulate radiation.  
* **Intelligence**  
  * Often \~50% of original intelligence at the expense of being way more dangerous.   
  * They are also more aggressive—including species commonly found to be defensive/amicable.  
* **Stats**  
  * **Reproduction**  
    * Often incapable.  
  * **Other**  
    * Explosive growth as the cells mutate.  
* **Soul Energy Capabilities**  
  * **Interface**  
    * Same in most cases  
  * **Skills**  
    * Often a more powerful version of the existing skills.

# 

# **Sand Worms**

* **Website Description**  
  * **Sand Worms** are native to the deserts around Gandew and must be the absolute worst creatures from that region\! They are always either burying themselves below the surface or cloaking themselves in plain sight, then viciously attacking unsuspecting victims. Luckily, Saynas often detect them, turning the tables and allowing adventurers to hunt them instead. It's crazy how some Darfaries still refuse to use Saynas and instead prefer to get high on the thrill of being attacked at any moment; it's pretty gruesome seeing a Darfaries and a Sand Worm duel to the death.  
* **Isekai History**  
  * A worm that burrows itself and attacks passersby. It is a relative of the Nari worms.  
  * They are the main source of Chitin in the kingdoms.  
* **Scientific History**  
  * Eunice aphroditois  
* **Habitat**  
  * Desert

* ## **Economic Value**

  * ### **Sand Worm Meat**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Butcher cuts meat. Not really desirable meat, so this is mostly sold as a by-product of killing Sand Worms for their chitin.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * ![][image2]

  * ### **Sand Worm Jerky**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Butcher prepared jerky. Not really desirable by many people due to its strong gamey flavor. Usually served up to poorer people.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * ![][image3]

  * ### **Sand Worm Chitin**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Butcher prepared Chitin for artisans to work on.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * See Above  
* **Tastes Like**  
  * Strong, Earthy, Nutty flavor.  
* **Alimentation**  
  * Smaller people, mammals, etc.   
  * Anything that moves and steps too close.  
* **Intelligence**  
  * Instinctual, bee-like.  
* **Stats**  
  * **Reproduction**  
    * Worms have both sexes.  
    * Every year, several worms are born  
  * **Other**  
    * Grows indefinitely at about \~10cm/Year, but most stop growing at this size due to caloric intake restrictions versus energy to predate.   
* **Soul Energy Capabilities**  
  * **Interface**  
    * Shell  
  * **Skills**  
    * They can harden parts of their bodies  
    * Generate Acid.  
    * Cloak  
    * Increase size,

# 

# **Walking {X} Flesh**

* **Website Description**  
  * **Walking Flesh** is the common name for any species stuck in this half-state thing. They are undead and seem to share some hive-mind cognition, but they don't seem completely devoid of their intelligence either. If they are taken far away from the castle, some transformed species will rampage against anything; some will idle, while some even demonstrate docile behaviors. The transformation compounds the behaviors, so the more they are transformed towards the walking-skin version, the more they lose whatever remains of their sanity. Don't underestimate their fighting capabilities; you might just become one of them.  
* **Isekai History**  
  * An endless amount of experimentation with what is now known as the Walking Heart Stone left tons of zombies of different species in Bahyum Castle. It somehow can create more    
* **Scientific History**  
  * The Return of the Living Dead Trioxin Zombie  
* **Habitat**  
  * Bahyum Castle, sometimes invading the Buhraies Plains

* ## **Economic Value**

  * ### **Extermination**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Usually, singular pieces of it (often the head) are torn off and brought back to prove the hunt was completed.  
    * **Cost**  
      * See Embassy Quests for more details.  
* **Tastes Like**  
  * Rotten dried meat.  
* **Alimentation**  
  * Soul Energy from Bahyum Castle  
* **Intelligence**  
  * Depends on experiment. Mindless\~Wolf  
* **Stats**  
  * **Reproduction**  
    * Through Soul Energy. All depends on Bayhum Castle  
  * **Other**  
    * None  
* **Soul Energy Capabilities**  
  * **Interface**  
    * Walking Heart  
  * **Skills**  
    * Slow Regen  
    * Halted Decomposition  
    * Decompose target  
    * Steal Soul Energy  
    * \+ Original skills.

# 

# **Walking {X} Skin**

* **Website Description**  
  * **Walking Skin,** sometimes called **Walking Bones,** is the name given to any species unlucky or dumb enough to have been sequestered by the Bahyum Castle creepy residents whenever they go on a rampage. Species that become walking skin seem to all act in a hive-mind manner, usually going for quantity over quality to kill anyone in their path. A good proportion of the walking skins are human. Some scientists believe it's just a matter of fact, given how many humans have been sequestered by the castle, but others believe the castle itself is breeding or somehow creating them through the walking heart stones. The Buhraies built the castle for soul experimentation, so expecting it to do creepy shit is not a crazy assumption—after all, the castle itself is alive and moody too. Just ask the most guards and adventurers in the local Bahyum village, and they will jokingly say it's moodier than a Wozefaries girl in her PMS.  
* **Isekai History**  
  * An endless amount of experimentation with what is now known as the Walking Heart Stone left tons of zombies in Bahyum Castle.   
  * They are slower and dumber than Walking Flesh.  
* **Scientific History**  
  * The Return of the Living Dead Trioxin Zombie  
* **Habitat**  
  * Bahyum Castle, sometimes invading the Buhraies Plains

* ## **Economic Value**

  * ### **Extermination**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Usually, singular pieces of it (often the Walking heart stone with the head bone) are torn off and brought back to prove the hunt was completed.  
    * **Cost**  
      * See Embassy Quests for more details.  
* **Tastes Like**  
  * Bone-dry, flaky skin.  
* **Alimentation**  
  * Soul Energy from Bahyum Castle  
* **Intelligence**  
  * Depends on experiment. Mindless\~Wolf  
* **Stats**  
  * **Reproduction**  
    * Impossible  
  * **Other**  
    * None  
* **Soul Energy Capabilities**  
  * **Interface**  
    * Walking Heart  
  * **Skills**  
    * Slow Regen  
    * Halted Decomposition  
    * Decompose target  
    * Steal Soul Energy

# 

# **Walking Flathead**

* **Website Description**  
  * **Walking Flathead,** or simply **Flathead.** All textbooks keep it simple: If you see one, you're fucked. At this point, everyone assumes they are the leaders of Bahyum Castle, serving as its physical manifestation. How many are there? Nobody knows. What species did they transform from? Nobody knows either. What some do know is that whatever the fuck this thing is, it must have been part of why Buhraies lost control of their own castle, which held countless scientific experiments, and likely also a factor that forced them to withdraw into Gandew Mountain. What adventurers and guards do know is that if you manage to kill one and drag its body to a seller, you can live like a king for a whole year. Odds are not on your side, so most likely, you will become walking flesh and then walking skin for eternity—think of the plus side\! You will become part of something greater than yourself\! Just like working a corporate job\!  
* **Isekai History**  
  * An endless amount of experimentation with what is now known as the Walking Heart Stone left a few Walking Flatheads in Bahyum Castle.   
  * They are incredibly hard to kill.  
* **Scientific History**  
  * Ghouls  
* **Habitat**  
  * Bahyum Castle

* ## **Economic Value**

  * ### **Walking Flathead Claws**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Often used for weapons or sharp tools.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * See above.

  * ### **Walking Flathead Tongue**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Often used for Nari gels.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * See above.  
* **Tastes Like**  
  * Bone.  
  * Dry flaky skin.  
* **Alimentation**  
  * Soul Energy from Bahyum Castle  
* **Intelligence**  
  * Lion  
* **Stats**  
  * **Reproduction**  
    * Attacking Flesh animals and laying worms on them.  
  * **Other**  
    * None.  
* **Soul Energy Capabilities**  
  * **Interface**  
    * Flat Head  
  * **Skills**  
    * Slow Regen  
    * Halted Decomposition  
    * See the Soul Energy of creatures.

# 

# **Wood People**

* **Website Description**  
  * **Wood People** were, amongst many other things, another one of Buhraie's experiments gone wrong—or right. The debate over what they were trying to achieve is still a hot topic, but historians at least agree that the Buhraies sought to make them into foot soldiers and a stable source of food and lumber. Meanwhile, scientists still can't fully wrap their heads around how they engineered a tree to be able to unearth itself and walk around in packs, let alone control its vines to defend itself. They did figure out that Buhraies used fost stones to paralyze and control Wood People based on specimens and tools found throughout the ages. Now they could be considered a plague as they are absolutely everywhere in the Buhraies Plains. Entire villages exist solely to strip these beasts for resources, with good prices going for their fantastic rubbery vines\! That is if you can cut them off, as they won't go down without a fight. If you get a beating, you can at least sink your teeth into a nearly head-sized tasty Wood People Yabuca to appease your emotional wounds.  
* **Isekai History**  
  * See World Timeline.  
  * Their blood/Sap is pink.  
  * Wood People’s bodies are used for all kinds of things, specifically buildings, but you gotta transport it somewhere to cut it down first.  
    * Or trick it to follow you like a herd.  
    * Otherwise, you are either using a river/stream to move the corpse, or using multiple eguas to carry a short distance (\< 100km)  
  * A barrier of Wood People can be formed by using Fost Stone jammed in their tops to paralyze them in place.  
* **Scientific History**  
  * Trees, kinda.  
  * It uses its vines, ground vibrations, and wind vibrations to map its surroundings. They are drawn to Fost stones because they can somewhat sense a direction in them.  
* **Habitat**  
  * Buhraies Plains.

* ## **Economic Value**

  * ### **Wood People Wood**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Has great durability and a dark red wood color  
      * Has a strong musk of woods if it gets soaked.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * ![][image4]

  * ### **Wood People Sap**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Processed sap, often used in Nari gels.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * ![][image5]

  * ### **Wood People Leaves**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Sun-dried, clean leaves mostly used for crafting.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * ![][image6]

  * ### **Wood People Vines**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Often used for ropes, constructions, etc. It is quite elastic, almost like rubber.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * ![][image7]  
* **Tastes Like**  
  * Wood  
* **Alimentation**  
  * Sun  
* **Intelligence**  
  * They are rather mindless, and so act purely on instinct. They only defend themselves by using their vine.  
  * They are drawn to Fost stones.  
  * However, if you shove a Fost Stone in the top of the trunk, you paralyze them.  
* **Stats**  
  * **Reproduction**  
    * Through cloning themselves by splitting right down the middle. However, they only tend to do it when there is enough space around them.  
  * **Other**  
    * Growth at \~30cm  
* **Soul Energy Capabilities**  
  * **Interface**  
    * Vines  
  * **Skills**  
    * Fast Strike (Causes a hole in the target’s flesh) by altering the physical properties of their vines.

# 

# **Hifuna People**

* **Website Description**  
  * **Hifuna People** are like the crazy cousin and are proof that Hifuna will fuck anyone up, including something as sturdy as a tree. They will slice and beat everything in their sight, including their own, to a mashed-potato consistency, then squat on top of it to absorb its nutrients. Wood People could also do that, but no one has seen them do it. It's a grim fate fighting these beasts; a Sapphire adventurer will have to be quick on its feet to evade how much soul energy Hifuna People pour into their interface—their attacks are much stronger than Wood People's, plus additional skills in the vines like making sonic booms, creating razor-sharp edges, and creating spikes that it launches towards you, not to be scoffed at. Don't forget the exploding branches too\! It can tear them off itself and chuck them toward you in desperation. On the plus side, Hifuna Yabucas sells well on the black market\! Not that we condone doing so, of course—  
* **Isekai History**  
  * See World Timeline.  
* **Scientific History**  
  * Trees, kinda.  
  * It uses its vines, ground vibrations, and wind vibrations to map its surroundings.   
  * It can also sense Soul energy using its leaves.  
* **Habitat**  
  * Hifuna

* ## **Economic Value**

  * ### **Hifuna People Wood**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Still has the same reddish tone to it, but it is plagued with Hifuna disease, so most people won’t use it for crafting.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * 

  * ### **Hifuna People Sap**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Processed sap is sometimes used in Nari gels.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * ![][image5]

  * ### **Hifuna People Leaves**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Sun-dried, clean leaves mostly used for crafting.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * 

  * ### **Hifuna People Vines**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Often used for ropes, constructions, etc. It is quite elastic, almost like rubber.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      *   
* **Tastes Like**  
  * Wood  
* **Alimentation**  
  * Sun, blood, and guts (more specifically, the soul energy within it.)  
* **Intelligence**  
  * They are rather mindless, and so act purely on instinct. They go out of their way to attack people if they see anything around, even other Hifuna People.  
  * However, if you shove a Fost Stone in the top of the trunk, you paralyze them.  
* **Stats**  
  * **Reproduction**  
    * Through cloning themselves by splitting right down the middle. However, they only tend to do it when there is enough space around them.  
  * **Other**  
    * Growth at \~30cm  
* **Soul Energy Capabilities**  
  * **Interface**  
    * Vines  
  * **Skills**  
    * Fast Strike (Causes a hole in the target’s flesh) by altering the physical properties of their vines.  
    * Their attacks are much stronger than Wood People's, plus additional skills in the vines like making sonic booms, creating razor-sharp edges, and even creating spikes that it launches towards you.   
  * **Interface**  
    * Branches  
  * **Skills**  
    * Don't forget the exploding branches too\! It can tear them off and chuck them toward you

# **Teefyro**

* **Website Description**  
  * **Teefyro** baffles scientists and causes many to disagree on whether this thing is a beast or fauna. It definitely lacks any formal intelligence as it rampages around aimlessly, sometimes even killing each other through the usual process of exploding its gums and sending its teeth as a projectile—sometimes killing itself in the process by bleeding to death, or in rarer cases, the interface controlling the explosion goes full haywire and detonates the entire body, leaving a crater where the beast once stood as a shower of blood and guts precipitates the whole area. It also randomly casts different spells through its flesh composition of the diverse fauna pieces and other beasts. Scientists still aren't sure how many combinations there are, but if it has something akin to teeth that explode outward, and its body shape resembles, or at least smells like, a Bagarisa, then for sure it's a Teefyro.  
* **Isekai History**  
  * ?  
* **Scientific History**  
  * ?  
* **Habitat**  
  * Hifuna

* ## **Economic Value**

  * ### **?**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * ?  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      *   
* **Tastes Like**  
  * ?  
* **Alimentation**  
  * ?  
* **Intelligence**  
  * ?  
* **Stats**  
  * **Reproduction**  
    * ?  
  * **Other**  
    * ?  
* **Soul Energy Capabilities**  
  * **Interface**  
    * Teeth  
  * **Skills**  
    * Teeth launch in front of him.

# **\--Defensive--**

# **Koalin**

* **Website Description**  
  * **Koalins** are truly a unique breed of beast; some scientists still dispute whether they should be categorized as beasts at all. They don’t really bite, claw, or kill people. What they do, however, is far worse\! Through the unique special wave generation organs in their head, alongside the white silky white beard that focuses those waves towards a target, they can strike virtually anyone with strong mental impairment. If they are just being defensive, they can make you feel a looming doom, a fearful sensation, but if they amp up the intensity, they can lead weak-minded people to kill themselves. If you resist that, they will increase the intensity to debilitating pain. There’s no winning against a Koalin. They are also nearly untamable, but if you manage to tame one, they are inseparable and will earn you reaches you never thought possible\!   
* **Isekai History**  
  * A cousin of the Soreno species.  
  * Rare cases of being tamed. Usually, babies are separated from their mothers who are retrieved by the hunter. It forms a bond with them and becomes a pet. Attempting to rebond with someone else is nearly impossible.  
* **Scientific History**  
  * Capybara  
* **Habitat**  
  * Lives throughout the lower lands. More often spotted in the grasslands to the East.

* ## **Economic Value**

  * ### **Koalin Meat**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Butcher cut meat.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * ![][image8]

  * ### **Koalin Jerky**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Butcher prepared jerky  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * ![][image9]

  * ### **Koalin Hide/Fur**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Butcher prepared hide/fur for artisans to work on.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * See above

  * ### **Koalin Brains \+ eyes \+ Moustache**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Used for Mind Enabler, which is illegal.   
      * If anyone is carrying these to sell, they will be brought in for questioning.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * See above.  
* **Tastes Like**  
  * Pork.  
* **Alimentation**  
  * Grass and some plants  
* **Intelligence**  
  * Capybara/Golden Retriever  
* **Stats**  
  * **Reproduction**	  
    * Female Koalins give birth every year to 5-7 babies.  
  * **Other**  
    * None  
* **Soul Energy Capabilities**  
  * **Interface**  
    * Beard (Male only)  
  * **Skills**  
    * Eye catch  
    * Bigger Looking  
    * Hypnosis

# 

# **Sweet Sting**

* **Website Description**  
  * **Sweet Sting** is found west of the Freeish Kingdom, with SweetLake being their HQ of sorts. They can be heard quite a way off, their wings making a deeper buzzing sound as they carry pollen or tree sap back to make black honey—an extremely nutritious yet diabetically sweet paste. It might sound threatening, but that's just a female worker passing by. They don't have stingers like their smaller male counterparts, but their pinchers are still quite powerful\! If you see black flames emerge from their pincers, get ready, as they are about to shoot a poisonous spit accelerated to near-sonic speeds by their soul-energy interface. Don't count on evading because they never seem to miss a shot\! There's even a common phrase in the local populace, *I'm sure as a spitting Sweet Sting.*  
* **Isekai History**  
  * A few Shapeshifters assimilated bees and began creating beehives to the west of Blood City. However, what makes them special is that they are much larger than normal bees, and they produce a black honey that is extremely tasty.  
  * Female bees use the sap of trees to make honey and build massive anthill-like constructs to store honey.  
  * If you get bit by the male sting, your sense of taste is overpowered with a sweet taste while you scream in agony.  
* **Scientific History**  
  * Megachile pluto  
* **Habitat**  
  * Sweetlake is the biggest nest.

* ## **Economic Value**

  * ### **Black Honey**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Black Honey is one of the best-tasting honeys in the kingdom. It is highly caloric, and some even say it is addictive.  
      * Often used to speed up Body Heal.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * **![][image10]**

  * ### **Sweet Sting Poison**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Often used for Nari gels.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * See Above  
* **Tastes Like**  
  * Nutty, smoky, slightly sweet.  
* **Alimentation**  
  * Nectar  
* **Intelligence**  
  * Instinctual and highly defensive of their natural spots.  
* **Stats**  
  * **Reproduction**  
    * Males are much smaller than females, and they do most of the work. Females defend the colony and gather pollen.  
  * **Other**  
    * None.  
* **Soul Energy Capabilities**  
  * **Interface**  
    * Mouth pinchers. Black flame emerges from the black stripes in the pinches.  
  * **Skills**  
    * Launch a copy of the pinchers forward.

# 

# **Crazed Muckman**

* **Website Description**  
  * **Crazed Muckman** is not as "crazy" or "man" as the name implies. They all come in all sorts of shapes and sizes, sometimes with extra feet, arms, or even triple the torso size, yet they all have a similar look and, most times, are not particularly aggressive—as long as they don't see you as food or you don't muck with them. Urban Folklore says these beasts are "born" when a shapeshifter eats a shitty bandit corpse, but scientists haven't been able to prove that. The current growing hypothesis is that these things are the—maybe natural—evolution of shapeshifters because of the environment around Blood and Freeish.  
* **Isekai History**  
  * **?**  
* **Scientific History**  
  * ?  
* **Habitat**  
  * West of blood into Freeish, rarely seen much farther out east of close to Forway.

* ## **Economic Value**

  * ### **?**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * ?  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      *   
* **Tastes Like**  
  * ?  
* **Alimentation**  
  * ?  
* **Intelligence**  
  * ?  
* **Stats**  
  * **Reproduction**  
    * ?  
  * **Other**  
    * ?  
* **Soul Energy Capabilities**  
  * **Interface**  
    * ?  
  * **Skills**  
    * ?

# 

# **Sucker Lady**

* **Website Description**  
  * **Sucker Lady** is exactly as the name states\! The origins are suspected to be similar to Crazed Muckman, with the difference being a shapeshifter eating a whore instead of a bandit. The jury is still out on whether this is a natural evolution of the shapeshifter in response to its environment, but it doesn't matter much in the end. What matters is that you should keep watch over your surroundings at night, especially if you start to feel followed. For some reason, they are picky with their targets, sometimes lurking around them for days before attacking. If you do get attacked, do know they will pin you down to suck at your soul through any interface they can, usually people's fingers since it's connected to the darfaries interface. You can use a fost lamp to drive them away, or give it something else finger-shaped to suck on...  
* **Isekai History**  
  * **?**  
* **Scientific History**  
  * ?  
* **Habitat**  
  * Hifuna

* ## **Economic Value**

  * ### **?**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * ?  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      *   
* **Tastes Like**  
  * ?  
* **Alimentation**  
  * ?  
* **Intelligence**  
  * ?  
* **Stats**  
  * **Reproduction**  
    * ?  
  * **Other**  
    * ?  
* **Soul Energy Capabilities**  
  * **Interface**  
    * ?  
  * **Skills**  
    * ?

# **Big Wingas**

* **Website Description**  
  * Big Wingas are beasts\! Say that to a room of scientists, and you surely spark conversations with a reasonable possibility of it evolving into a shouting fest or maybe, just maybe, a full brawl. They are another result of Buhraies messing with wingas and flingas, at least that's reasonable to expect, as the likelihood of this thing coming into existence on its own is pretty low, given their ancestry split off into their distinct taxonomic family quite a while back. What is curious about these creatures is that their intellect is advanced enough that some scientists will put their reputations on the line to say they are a race like the cruros, not a beast like the bruntos. Others say it opens the door to a slippery slope, and soon nari worms are a race too, and being given badges to become citizens, and then everyone is fucked because grounded nari prices will go up because nari worms will control the market.   
  * Rants amongst scientists aside, big wingas are unfortunately near extinct, if not outright extinct, in the wild for all intended purposes. Their delicious meat and beautiful, large feathers led people to hunt them down until the big wingas somehow survived and learned to eschew everything and everyone.  
  * However, families of people who stole eggs and raised them instead are now swimming in balls selling nari gel of them. They don't really sell adults for others because they grow so attached to people, but if you put in the time and effort, you can buy a big winga egg and raise one yourself—if you have the balls to pay for one, and if you can even find one as this species is not known for being the frisky type.  
  * At least it's not rare to see their beauty with your own eyes, as you can see Forway guards using them, but that's a guard wearing nari gel, so don't expect them to act bird-like. It is safely assumed they carefully preserve their flesh and replicate nari gels from it, or that they have a breeding stock nobody knows about. Either way, they are tight-lip about it.  
* **Isekai History**  
  * Much larger Wingas that lived in the Wingarest. Were physically manipulated versions of the Wingas used by the Buhraies to fly around. Some of them still exist, but are a rare sight as they grew fearful of humans who hunted them down to near extinction.  
  * Their meat does not taste as sweet as Winga meat as Big developed a taste for meat as well.  
  * They are commonly found with Forway guards, but that’s a guard wearing nari gel.  
* **Scientific History**  
  * Macaw  
* **Habitat**  
  * Rarely seen throughout the four kingdoms. Now often using Wingarest to live.  
  * Forway uses them for 

* ## **Economic Value**

  * ### **Big Winga Meat**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Butcher cut meat.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * ![][image11]

  * ### **Big Winga Jerky**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Butcher prepared jerky  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * ![][image12]

  * ### **Big Winga Feathers**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Butcher prepared feathers for artisans to work on.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * See above

  * ### **Big Winga Egg**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * A treat for many people due to its sweet taste.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * ![][image13]

  * ### **Big Winga Tamed** 

    * **Isekai History**  
      * A great hunter companion for Aquamarine adventurers, or as a pet for richer people. Rare to find, and costly to raise, but at least they live long.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * See above  
* **Tastes Like**  
  * Same as Winga  
* **Alimentation**  
  * Same as Winga, but they also eat bigger fruits, specifically green bread.  
* **Intelligence**  
  * Same as Winga  
* **Stats**  
  * **Reproduction**  
    * \~3 or 4 pairs of Winga couples get together for a swing party, then they all build a shared nest to care for the eggs. It takes the entire summer for them to hatch.  
    * Every year around 5 Big Winga eggs are laid.  
  * **Other**  
    * None.  
* **Soul Energy Capabilities**  
  * **Interface**  
    * Head feathers  
  * **Skills**  
    * Generate Light  
    * Sense Lifeforms  
    * Generate Air Shield

# 

# 

# **Trunk People**

* **Website Description**  
  * **Trunk People** are native to heavy, thick forests around the Freeish Kingdom. They are easily recognizable by their four arms, each able to display different-colored flames and alter their intensity. The name *"Trunk People"* is an old one, for scientists now know they are nothing but parasites\! Still, they are cute and can even recognize faces. You see them everywhere in Freeish and surrounding communities as public light sources and even as security for some because their flaming and blinding capabilities are pretty impressive for their size. Some can also respond to certain body motions and training, often becoming part of shows and traveling bands.  
* **Isekai History**  
  * A mutated tree branch that sparkled life. They are very protective of their surroundings and will use their interface if they feel threatened.  
  * They communicate only by casting different colors of light. They cannot hear.  
  * Their hair is pollen.  
  * They live in trunks of Live Wood trees.  
  * Often used by people in the Freeish kingdom and around as a public light source on large Soul Wood trees.  
  * Some use it inside their homes as well.  
  * \*Usage as a seed to plant wherever the owner wants the tree.  
* **Scientific History**  
  * Mutated Tree Branches  
* **Habitat**  
  * South West

* ## **Economic Value**

  * ### **Trunk People Hair**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Used on Nari gels.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * See Above

  * ### **Trunk People Arms**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Used on Nari.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * See Above  
* **Tastes Like**  
  * Wood  
* **Alimentation**  
  * Tree sap.  
* **Intelligence**  
  * Monkey  
  * They can plan ahead, attack, defend, form families, recognize people, and communicate with others.  
* **Stats**  
  * **Reproduction**  
    * Flower in the head with pollen.  
    * Every Summer  
  * **Other**  
    * None.  
* **Soul Energy Capabilities**  
  * **Interface**  
    * Arm branches  
  * **Skills**  
    * Light Flames  
    * Light Explosion (blind)

# 

# **Shapeshifter**

* **Website Description**  
  * **Shapeshifters** are presumed to be one of the last remaining ancient species in this world. They are intelligent, have forethought capabilities, have an interface with a wide range of actions, and can even communicate in the shape of the creature they are mimicking. However, a growing group of scientists is classifying it as a beast instead, as all of its apparent intelligence is just another form of mimicry because, at the end of the day, its communication skills show that it is nothing other than a mindless blob, seeking to grow large enough to divide itself in half. Or the shapeshifters that got captured are the dumb ones. Who knows? Although tavern gossip about it floats around once in a while, such as a specific shapeshifter that more than once approached lonely male adventurers with a distinct hole in the middle that they felt was perfectly lubricated and warm. Some seemed flustered when asked how they knew that, while others stated they interacted with it *recreationally.*  
* **Isekai History**  
  * One of the last remaining ancient species experiments in this world occurred when one of the ancient species tested rocks in the forest.  
  * Many years later, the rocks began to grow and develop a jelly-like appearance. Many years later some of these rocks began to assimilate nearby living beings and transform into them while others began to assimilate with themselves to share experiences.  
* **Scientific History**  
  * Bryozoan Colony \- Natural Appearance  
* **Habitat**  
  * Mostly west of Blood City

* ## **Economic Value**

  * ### **Jelly Blob**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Usually a part of the Shapeshifter that has been cut off, and put on a glass for transport. More expensive because they're hard to catch.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * See Above  
* **Tastes Like**  
  * Slightly salty jelly.  
* **Alimentation**  
  * Dead matter. Opportunistic.  
* **Intelligence**  
  * Rather instinctual, but slowly developing a sense of being. Some of these rocks are intelligent enough to understand some words from adventurers.  
* **Stats**  
  * **Reproduction**  
    * They can duplicate themselves at the cost of halving their mass, but most rocks combine themselves with other rocks first then halve their mass. That way they can share their life experiences.  
  * **Other**  
    * Maybe it is a single life-form entity, but each piece is trying to act independently to gather information.  
* **Soul Energy Capabilities**  
  * **Interface**  
    * Internal Organs  
  * **Skills**  
    * Transmogrify

# 

# **Monica Swarm**

* **Website Description**  
  * ?  
* **Isekai History**  
  * A swarm of Monarch butterflies that groups up and flees away from bad weather. The combination of their blue lights creates a wall of blue in the distance, signaling to anyone in the way to protect themselves from the incoming storm.  
  * They can devour a rotting corpse in a few hours, depending on the size of the swarm.  
* **Scientific History**  
  * Butterfly Swarm  
* **Habitat**  
  * Lower plains

* ## **Economic Value**

  * None. See Fauna \> Monica  
* **Tastes Like**  
  * See Fauna \> Monica  
* **Alimentation**  
  * See Fauna \> Monica  
* **Intelligence**  
  * Butterfly  
* **Stats**  
  * **Reproduction**  
    * Attacking certain trees for their sap.  
  * **Other**  
    * None.  
* **Soul Energy Capabilities**  
  * **Interface**  
    * See Fauna \> Monica.  
  * **Skills**  
    * None

# **\--Amicable--**

# **Flinga**

* **Website Description**  
  * **Flingas** are massive beasts that fly high up in the sky, rarely ever coming close to the ground. Nobody knows where these beasts nest their younglings—if they even do that. Their complete lack of migratory patterns and sometimes random flight patterns, such as flying over a specific city for days, still baffles scientists and historians. There are tales by the campfire surrounding a sapphire adventurer who wore Big Winga skin to fly back home, and on the way, he saw what appeared to be a child-sized wood humanoid riding on top of it. Some Scientists suspect it was a child of an ancient species called Fuhraies; others state it could be Finaies, another ancient species rumored to live north of Hifuna. Attempting to capture a Flinga is also damn nearly impossible as not only do they quickly climb higher at any sight of danger, but they can also use their soul energy interface to stun enemies with a few attacks, including unfathomably high-pitched screams and air gust streams.  
* **Isekai History**  
  * Big flying birds that are only seen flying up in the air except during winter (under most years). Nobody knows how they eat, sleep, etc.  
    * \*Nobody except for Finaies. See World Timeline  
* **Scientific History**  
  * Pteranodon  
* **Habitat**  
  * Seeing flying around the entire region.

* ## **Economic Value**

  * ### **Flinga Meat**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Butcher cut meat. A refined, rare product that some people in Forway insist on having even though there is nothing special about it.  
    * **Cost**  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * ![][image11]

  * ### **Flinga Jerky**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Butcher prepared jerky. A refined, rare product that some people in Forway insist on having even though there is nothing special about it.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * ![][image12]

  * ### **Flinga Head Feathers**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Bundle of head feathers used for Nari gel.  
      * Due to how hard it is to get,   
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * See above

  * ### **Flinga Egg**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Some eggs are rarely found, but Humans/Darfaries/etc. were never able to hatch them.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * ![][image14]

  * ### **Flinga Tamed** 

    * **Isekai History**  
      * No one in the four kingdoms has managed to tame a Flinga yet.   
      * Managing to tame one would greatly impact transportation and therefore, costs, expansion.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * See above  
* **Tastes Like**  
  * Tough Chicken  
* **Alimentation**  
  * Large Berries/Fruits  
* **Intelligence**  
  * Dolphin  
* **Stats**  
  * **Reproduction**  
    * Every year around 2 macaws eggs are laid.  
  * **Other**  
    * None.  
* **Soul Energy Capabilities**  
  * **Interface**  
    * Head feathers  
  * **Skills**  
    * Generate Light  
    * Sense Lifeforms  
    * Generate Air Shield

# 

# **Nari Worms**

* **Website Description**  
  * **Nari Worms** are native to the underground caverns below Blood. Despite their threatening look and massive size, they are quite amicable to everyone, even when you find their eggs. Avoid the curiosity of eating them or harming these worms in any way, or you will be in deep shit if Forway finds out. They are so protective of these creatures that they are not afraid to use the worst punishment in the book, including being shipped out to Hifuna. It is certainly harsh, but it is fair as nari stones are a byproduct from the shed skin of Nari Worms, and no other creature or scientific process can accumulate this much soul energy in a ready-to-use form.  
* **Isekai History**  
  * With the lack of predators, this worm began to grow large and specialize in burrowing and eating stone using an organ that casts Soul Energy through a unique interface.  
  * The acids in its stomach mix with the existing hair-strand-sized Nari veins into its shell, which it replaces every few months as it grows bigger.   
  * Humans hunt down the shells left behind and ground them for various uses.  
  * Killing them is prohibited by Forway/Blood law  
* **Scientific History**  
  * Worms  
* **Habitat**  
  * Underground around the mountains of Blood city. See Nari Caverns for more information

* ## **Economic Value**

  * ### **Nari Worm Meat**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Butcher cut meat. Horrible taste.   
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * ![][image15]

  * ### **Nari Worm Jerky**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Butcher prepared jerky. Not really desirable.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * ![][image3]

  * ### **Nari Worm Chitin**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Chitin from when Nari worms shed their skin.   
      * When heated, the smell coming from it is slightly oily, with a mildewy, earthy finish—like a distant cousin of cockroaches.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * ![][image16]

  * ### **Nari Worm Egg**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * A rare find with tons of Soul Energy built into it.   
      * It replenishes about Tier III while acting as Nari Refresher as well.  
      * Is somewhat delicate and will rather randomly hatch to a tiny Nari worm.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * ![][image17]

  * ### **Nari Stones**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * See Nari Caverns  
      * Because some species do not have enough Soul Energy within their bodies for higher tier spells, they use the energy stored within the Nari stones to cast Soul Energy.  
      * Forway considers this stone of utmost importance to the balance of life. Transport costs are waived, as adventurers take responsibility to move such important goods.  
      * Upon grinding the Nari worm shell, it is heated up into small stones that recombine into a crystalline, diamond-like shape. It is then inserted into Soul wood and polished to give it a pointed edge for easier stacking.  
      * Nari Stones can be chained into one another to hasten spell cast time, and storage.  
      * Upon use, the stone becomes diamond-like as the blue hue fades. Some chuck it in the ground as waste, but most people sell it in bulk for it to be melted into alternative glass that feels somewhat plasticky and is less translucent.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * ![][image18]  
* **Tastes Like**  
  * Metallic, earthy.  
* **Alimentation**  
  * Rocks.  
* **Intelligence**  
  * Comparable to a snake. It is mostly instinctual, but responds to certain foods.  
  * Communicate through pheromones and sound.  
* **Stats**  
  * **Reproduction**  
    * Due to their size and longevity they rarely reproduce and generate offspring. When they do, it is at most three or four and it takes months for them to hatch.  
  * **Other**  
    * Grows indefinitely, but seems to stop around this height due to caloric requirements.  
* **Soul Energy Capabilities**  
  * **Interface**  
    * Shell  
  * **Skills**  
    * They can harden parts of their bodies  
    * Generate Acid.

# 

# **Bruntos**

* **Website Description**  
  * **Bruntos** are a glimpse of the endless curiosity and tampering mindset of the ancient Buhraies race. Not only content in messing with plants and the land itself, but they were also as far south as Endlessfog, altering the local ecosystem to their will until Lishies wrecked the place up and tried to eat anything that moved in the whole island. That's what historians say, but scientists are still perplexed about how they created an entirely new species out of thin air. Nothing close to Bruntos other than Naked Bruntos also called humans, existed—not even in Newland. Some scientists speculate that this is substantial evidence that our physical bodies are interconnected with a soul plane, and that other planes of existence are also connected to this shared soul plane—this must be how Buhraies connected flesh to the soul and brought us all here. Other scientists say these scientists need to lay off the Magina and stop getting high on the job. Either way, Bruntos makes great companions\!  
* **Isekai History**  
  * Brought over by Buhraies as summoning experiments, but were left to their own demise soon after into what is now Endlessfog.  
  * As their population expanded, so did their tribes and the number of wars until a single Brunto led an army of conquest and unified all tribes under a single leadership.  
    * Now represented in the Endlessfog statue. See the city history for more info.  
    * Killing them is prohibited by Endlessfog law. Does not apply to greater Forway, especially the Freeish Region.  
* **Scientific History**  
  * Chimpanzee  
* **Habitat**  
  * Endlessfog island.   
  * Freeish Region (some escaped into the wild through boat commerce.)

* ## **Economic Value**

  * ### **Bruntos Meat**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Butcher cut meat.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * ![][image15]  
* **Tastes Like**  
  * Not tender, and somewhat greasy. Also kinda bland.  
* **Alimentation**  
  * Omnivorous, insects, fruits, etc.  
* **Intelligence**  
  * Chimpanzee. They haven’t discovered the fire yet, but make use of some tools, specially if trained by humans.  
  * Great for adventurers who need a cargo mate in very rough terrain.  
* **Stats**  
  * **Reproduction**  
    * Throughout the year, more during Summer.  
  * **Other**  
    * None  
* **Soul Energy Capabilities**  
  * **Interface**  
    * None  
  * **Skills**  
    * None

# 

# **White Roundies**

* **Website Description**  
  * **White Roundies** are gentle mammals from the far south known as Whiteland, often coming up to the shores of Freeish but sometimes as far as Sanbi during the winter to feast on their heart, given the winter in Whiteland is far colder than the Four Kingdoms' winter. Don't let their cute nature fool you, though\! They are incredibly intelligent, with some scientists dedicating their lives to investigating their culture alone. It is commonly accepted that this beast is likely on track to become its own race in the far future, but people disagree on what the turning point is. For some, the fact we can't understand their language is the most significant differentiator from Cruros. Others fight that argument on the grounds that they might be even more intelligent than us, and we don't understand them. Others think this whole back-and-forth is a waste of time and prefer to play catch, rub their bellies to hear the rumbling noise that sounds like a pur, made with their neck sensory tentacles, or straight up make companions out of them. If you're one of these people, there's a whole festival around their arrival in Freeish Kingdom you cannot miss out on.  
* **Isekai History**  
  * A seal-like mammal that lives in the far south most of the year.  
  * Often killed during the winter when they move to northern waters during winter to fatten up for spring.  
  * Usually frowned upon by most people as they seem too intelligent, specifically people like-minded to endlessfog residents.  
* **Scientific History**  
  * Seal/Penguin  
* **Habitat**  
  * Whiteland for most of the year, Four kingdoms shores during the Winter to eat tons of fish before they go back south to procreate during Spring.

* ## **Economic Value**

  * ### **White Roundies Meat**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Some people like it, but eating it is often considered weird because of its unusual taste.  
      * Way more expensive/unavailable except for winter.  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * ![][image19]

  * ### **White Roundies Hair Strands**

    * **Isekai History**  
      * Used for Nari gel  
    * **Visual Reference**  
      * See above  
* **Tastes Like**  
  * Fishy, duck-like.  
* **Alimentation**  
  * Fish  
* **Intelligence**  
  * Much higher than a dog, with a very specific kind of intelligence. Does have their own cultures, and social norms. They can’t speak/understand Vraki, but they can understand actions, such as pointing to the sea while holding a big slice of meat. They might seem to exchange items randomly, but the better the meat, the higher chances they will try to payback the goodness.  
    * Sometimes they make odd vocal sounds that sound like Vraki. It’s like they are trying to learn, or atl east repeat back what they heard from people talking.  
* **Stats**  
  * **Reproduction**  
    * Every year in Spring after they fatten up on the warmer water during winter.  
  * **Other**  
    * None  
* **Soul Energy Capabilities**  
  * **Interface**  
    * The hair around their necks vibrates, creating a rumbling sound when above water.  
  * **Skills**  
    * They can use Soul Energy to detect things around them  
    * Create sound blast waves that can deafen anyone nearby. They use that to stun their prey.

#
